title: DP-lite action-value features: `pit_now` vs `wait_1` vs `wait_2`
summary: Operations-research work on F1 strategy models pit timing as a sequential decision problem, not a single threshold rule.
rationale: Operations-research work on F1 strategy models pit timing as a sequential decision problem, not a single threshold rule. Your best code already has heuristic `Wait1` features; a stronger version is to compute approximate action values from empirical degradation and pit-loss curves learned from the data.
implementation_hint: Fit simple race-local pace/degradation templates by compound and tyre age using train rows after excluding immediate pit laps. Then simulate a tiny decision tree for each row: total expected remaining cost under `pit_now`, `wait_1`, and `wait_2`, including out-lap warmup and feasible compound choices. Feed value gaps like `Q_wait1-Q_pit`, `best_future_compound`, and `pit_now_regret` to the booster.
expected_effect: Converts hand-built pressures into a coherent short-horizon control signal and may improve cases where the model must choose between pitting slightly early and slightly late.
risk: Bad empirical pace templates will inject structured error; if the simulator is too detailed it becomes brittle and duplicates prior heuristic noise.
